cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out why smog became a common concern based on the given text. The question is asking for the reason, so I need to look for the part of the passage that explains why smog is a common concern. The passage mentions that smog is especially a common concern and then says, "As a popular online post said, air may be the only thing that is equal for everyone, despite your income or profession." The key part here is that "air may be the only thing that is equal for everyone." This implies that regardless of how much money you have or what job you have, everyone breathes the same air. So, no matter your status, you can't avoid the smog. Looking at the options, option D says "Because nobody can avoid it." This seems to align with the text's explanation. The other options might not be the best fit. Option A talks about higher prices, but the text says people with higher incomes can pay for better water and food, but not air. Option B mentions a popular online post discussing it, but the reason it's a concern isn't just because it was discussed; it's because of the equality of exposure. Option
